question.text:
CASE STUDY: Mr. Sharma, aged 45, is in the 30% income tax bracket. He has the following mutual fund investments during FY 2025-26:
Investment 1: Equity MF - Invested Rs. 5,00,000, held for 15 months, sold at a profit of Rs. 3,00,000. Investment 2: Debt MF - Purchased in February 2024, invested Rs. 4,00,000, held for 8 months, sold at a profit of Rs. 50,000. Investment 3: ELSS Fund - Invested Rs. 1,50,000, held for 4 years, sold at a profit of Rs. 80,000. Investment 4: Received IDCW of Rs. 8,000 from an equity MF scheme during the year.
Question 1: What is the nature of the capital gain on Mr. Sharma's equity MF investment (Investment 1) and the applicable tax rate?

answer.text: Long-term capital gain taxed at 12.5% under Section 112A (holding period of 15 months exceeds the 12-month threshold for equity MFs)
Short-term capital gain taxed at 20% under Section 111A (15 months is short-term for equity)
Long-term capital gain taxed at 10% under Section 112A (old rate applies to pre-July 2024 purchases)
Long-term capital gain taxed at investor's slab rate of 30% (equity MFs taxed at slab rate)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For equity-oriented mutual funds, the holding period threshold for LTCG is 12 months. Mr. Sharma held his equity MF units for 15 months (>12 months), so this qualifies as LTCG. Under Section 112A, equity LTCG is taxed at 12.5% (post-Budget 2024 rate). The first Rs. 1,25,000 of equity LTCG in a financial year is exempt.
**The Trap:** Option B wrongly classifies 15 months as short-term for equity. Option C uses the old 10% rate which was replaced by 12.5% effective July 23, 2024. Option D applies slab rate which is for debt funds, not equity.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The 12-month threshold for equity and 12.5% LTCG rate are fundamental to equity MF taxation. Always verify the holding period against the correct threshold for the fund type.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Mr. Sharma benefited from holding beyond 12 months - his 20% STCG rate dropped to 12.5% LTCG rate, plus he gets the Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption. A 3-month difference saved significant tax.
